serviceDetail.label: Service
serviceDetail.workTitle: Projects that show how we work

direction.lede: Visual strategy and direction — from brand concept to consistent execution across every touchpoint.
direction.processTitle: Four steps — from idea to execution

01.title: Understand the brand
01.desc: A short, sharp brief that surfaces tone, ambition and milestones. I don't guess — I ask.

02.title: Set the direction
02.desc: Strategy, mood, first visual language. Everything else follows from this.

03.title: Lead the execution
03.desc: I work with a team (designers, photographers, developers) — as the conductor.

04.title: Keep it consistent
04.desc: Brand book, systems, guidelines, so the brand keeps working without me.

direction.whyTitle: Why trust me with creative direction?

01.title: I see the whole
01.desc: From logo to campaign copy — I think about design and communication at the same time.

02.title: I lead teams
02.desc: I work with developers, photographers and writers — projects run without translation loss.

03.title: Lasting identity
03.desc: I don't chase trends. Every brand I've built still works today.

branding.lede: Identity systems — logo, palette, typography, voice and brand book that make a brand recognisable.
branding.processTitle: Branding isn't just a logo. It's a system

01.title: Understand the story
01.desc: Who are you? Who are you speaking to? Why will they remember you?

02.title: Build the mark
02.desc: Logo, palette, typography — brand DNA in a few elements.

03.title: Extend the system
03.desc: How the brand performs on a business card, poster, screen, package.

04.title: Pack it into a book
04.desc: Brand book or CGP — so the team can continue without me.

01.title: System, not just a picture
01.desc: Logo is the start. The system is what keeps a brand recognisable for years.

02.title: Collaborative
02.desc: The brand doesn't come from my taste. It comes from your goals, values and understanding of your customers — the process is personal.

03.title: Print + digital + product
03.desc: From the start I think about how the brand will work across all media — not separately for each channel.

graphic.lede: Editorial layouts, visual communication, campaign visuals — design that carries content.
graphic.processTitle: Design that doesn't overpower the message

01.title: Brief and content
01.desc: First I understand what I'm communicating, then I design.

02.title: Sketches and grid
02.desc: Hierarchy, grid, typography — the foundation of the layout.

04.title: Print or screen
04.desc: I prepare files for print (CMYK) or web (responsive).

01.title: Editorial precision
01.desc: From the 'More good restaurants' book to Molly Lolly — long-form experience.

02.title: Campaigns that hold up
02.desc: Not just pretty — consistent across many formats.

03.title: I understand print
03.desc: Working with printers since 2008. I know what colour does on paper.

marketing.lede: A strategic approach to communication — campaigns that connect brands with people at the right moment.
marketing.processTitle: Marketing without throwing ideas at the wall

01.title: Understand the goal
01.desc: What are we measuring? Brand awareness, sales, loyalty? No campaign without it.

02.title: Find the story
02.desc: What makes this brand different? Here the campaign concept is born.

03.title: Execution across channels
03.desc: Print, digital, social, TV — one message, adapted per medium.

04.title: Measure and adjust
04.desc: I don't guess what works. I look at the data and adapt.

01.title: Brand + marketing together
01.desc: A campaign isn't separate from identity — with me you get both connected.

02.title: National campaign experience
02.desc: Petrol, mBills, Vsestoritve — TV, billboards, social, all firsthand.

03.title: Audience understanding
03.desc: I don't communicate what I want to say — I communicate what the audience will get.

web.lede: Websites and apps that combine story, motion and precision — from concept to live launch.
web.processTitle: From sketch to live launch — without lost translations

01.title: Discovery and strategy
01.desc: Who are the users? What do they need to achieve? What tone, what tempo?

02.title: UX + UI design
02.desc: Wireframes, prototypes in Figma — before any code. Saves weeks of work.

03.title: Development with my team
03.desc: I work with trusted developers (Next.js, React, GSAP). I lead so the build stays on brand.

04.title: Launch and support
04.desc: QA, performance, SEO basics. After launch I stay on for tweaks and long-term care.

web.whyTitle: Why entrust your web to Pinart.

01.title: Apps and websites — from CMS to native code
01.desc: On the web I work with WordPress and Shopify for catalogs, blogs and e-commerce, through Webflow and Framer for design-driven brochure sites, all the way to Next.js, React, GSAP and headless CMS systems (Sanity, Contentful) when the project demands full control. For apps, from FlutterFlow for quick MVPs, through React Native and Flutter for cross-platform, to Swift and Kotlin for native iOS/Android. Together we pick the stack based on budget, team and long-term maintenance.

02.title: I lead the team, not just sketch
02.desc: I understand how developers think and can translate design into code in shared language.

03.title: Brand-first build
03.desc: Every detail — animation, micro-interaction, font fallback — reflects the brand. Not just function.

ideas.lede: Strategic concept development — from the first spark to an executable idea we can actually build.
ideas.processTitle: Ideas that survive the path from thought to execution

01.title: Shared thinking session
01.desc: Questions, challenges, context. Best ideas don't come alone — they emerge in conversation.

02.title: Diverge
02.desc: Many directions, no censorship. Many bad ones to reach a great one.

03.title: Converge
03.desc: Pick the strongest. Develop it into an executable concept.

04.title: Prepare for execution
04.desc: Brief, visuals, plan. Any team that gets it knows what to do next.

01.title: My own projects prove it
01.desc: Molly Lolly, Wish & Vibe — ideas I developed from scratch into actual products.

02.title: Strategy + aesthetics
02.desc: An idea is only strong if you can pitch and execute it. With me you get both.

03.title: Honesty
03.desc: If the idea doesn't work, I'll tell you. One good idea beats five half-baked ones.

about.body: I create digital experiences that combine storytelling, movement and atmosphere. Together with developers and creatives, I build modern websites, branding systems and visual experiences for brands that want character.
about.headline: When an idea gets shape, rhythm and space

clients.heading: Clients
clients.desc: Working with founders, teams and organisations to create brands, products and digital experiences that connect with people. Each collaboration is shaped by strategy, creativity and a shared ambition to build something meaningful.

items.inovis: The redesign of the website and visual identity gave Inovis IT d.o.o. a more recognisable and professional presence. Tina understood our needs and translated them into a visual identity and digital experience that represents us much better today.
items.inovisTitle: A modern identity for a modern company
items.mbills: With Tina, we redesigned the mBills user experience and online presence. The results exceeded expectations — we more than doubled our new customers after the redesign. Her ability to connect business goals with user experience was key to the project's success.
items.mbillsTitle: The mBills redesign more than doubled our customer growth
items.medipedi: For MediPedi, Tina created a complete visual identity and professional photography. The result is a recognisable, cohesive identity that reflects expertise, trust and care for clients. Her attention to detail made a real difference.
items.medipediTitle: A brand that reflects the quality of our service
items.rekruter: Tina designed a complete visual identity for Rekruter that perfectly reflects our values. From the start she understood our vision and translated it into a modern, professional visual system. We're extremely satisfied with the result.
items.rekruterTitle: A new identity for a growing brand

contact.headline: Let's create something memorable
contact.body1: Together, we can create solutions that inspire and stand out.
contact.body2: Let's combine our creative strengths and make something extraordinary. Feel free to reach out — I look forward to working with you!

lucky7.heroLede: From a basic landing page into a gaming experience that invites the player in — a redesign of a classic online game for the Slovenian lottery.
lucky7.briefTitle:
Build a web game

that calls you to play.
lucky7.briefBody1: Lucky 7 is a classic lottery game that lived online as a static page — it worked, but nothing pulled you in. The task: rebuild the visual language so the game actually looks like a game — modern, gaming-style, with animations that nudge the player to click.
lucky7.briefBody2: Approach: moodboards → style explorations → direction pick → mobile + desktop UI → motion prototypes.
lucky7.productLede: Responsive redesign — from a big screen at home to a phone on the bus. Every screen with its own gaming energy.
lucky7.explorationLede: We prepared three colour directions — a violet with neon accents, a blue with glassy gloss, and a gold-on-dark premium variant. I was rooting for the blue middle one, but the client wanted to stay within the existing violet-and-gold identity.
lucky7.motionLede: The real character of this redesign lives in motion — how the seven vibrates, how the cards open up, how the background responds to a click. That's what brings the player back.
lucky7.statMoodboardsDesc: Style explorations for the first direction pick.
lucky7.statDirectionDesc: Within the brand palette, in a more modern voice.

mollyLolly.heroLede: A whole brand from zero — into books, plush, AR app, 3D room and an e-shop — one vision, one direction, multiple teams.
mollyLolly.metaRoleValue: Creative Direction · Brand Owner · Product Lead
mollyLolly.introTitle: A brand that doesn't stay in Figma.
mollyLolly.introBody1: Molly Lolly shows how I work when someone hires me as a brand manager or creative director: I don't just draw a logo and send a PDF — I run the brand from concept to execution.
mollyLolly.introBody2: Here I was the author, the client, the project lead and the creative director all at once. The illustrations, books, plush and the full UX/UI of the digital products I designed myself. For the app development, AR and 3D environment I hired developers — and led them until every piece matched the brand.
mollyLolly.introBody3: The result isn't a moodboard. The result is a child's world, where they can pick up a book, prop it against a tablet, and see the characters step off the page.
mollyLolly.metaMyRoleBody:
Brand creator

Creative direction

Illustration

Product & book design

UX & UI design

Vendor & team management
mollyLolly.metaAudienceSub: and the parents reading with them
mollyLolly.universeLede: I grew one brand into a physical and digital family — all connected, all speaking the same language, all created under my direction.
mollyLolly.capActivityDesc: Draw & Solve — 80 stickers, tasks, colouring pages.
mollyLolly.capPlushDesc: Plush monkey and a wider textile collection (clothing, hats, slippers).
mollyLolly.booksLede: Each book is a warm tale with a happy ending and a quiet pedagogical layer. Hardcover 25×25 cm, printed in Slovenia.
mollyLolly.plushLede: Plush Molly Lolly — minimal, soft, made in Slovenia. Alongside the toy, a textile collection with clothes, hats and slippers for kids.
mollyLolly.digitalTitle: A book that comes alive in a child's hands.
mollyLolly.digitalLede: Molly Lolly's digital world is not just one app. It's several connected products I designed and directed — the code was delivered by a team I led.
mollyLolly.product1Desc: The main product: it recognises the book page and overlays animated characters, voice and interaction.
mollyLolly.product2Desc: When there's not enough light for AR, the app switches into a 3D room — the child still walks the character around and visits a book that opens its digital version.
mollyLolly.product3Desc: A memory game with characters from the books — reinforcing the content through play.
mollyLolly.product4Desc: Pages from the activity workbooks, brought to touch on screen.
mollyLolly.commerceTitle: A store where it all comes together.
mollyLolly.commerceLede: An e-commerce with playful design — books, plush, bundles. All the characters, all the colours, one brand.
mollyLolly.statProductsDesc: 2 books, 2 workbooks, plush, AR app, 3D, games.
mollyLolly.statVisionDesc: All from one hand, every team led by me.
mollyLolly.statUniverseDesc: A brand that grows with every new story.

petrolPay.heroLede: It started with a logo. It ended with a family of cards that shine in your pocket and a portal where you order them in just a few clicks.
petrolPay.introTitle:
A new payment brand

for Slovenia's largest fuel retailer.
petrolPay.introBody1: Petrol Pay is Petrol's own card scheme — a payment card and loyalty programme in one, from the personal Loyalty card to Klub and Business tiers.
petrolPay.introBody2: The task: build a visual identity that breathes with the parent brand but stands on its own. Logo, palette, typography, six card proposals — one of which went into production — and an ordering portal with SVG animations and a short intro video.
petrolPay.identityTitle:
A mark that reads from a pump

and from a phone
petrolPay.familyLede: Six proposals, one pick — Gold for everyone driving their own way. Klub for the loyal. Business for those driving for work.
petrolPay.stat1Desc: Card concepts developed, from minimal to iridescent. One was picked.
petrolPay.stat2Desc: Pages of the complete brand guidelines for the mark and its use.
petrolPay.stat3Desc: UX and UI screens for the PKPK portal — ordering, registration, support.
petrolPay.explorationLede: The client picked the vertical direction, but along the way these variants were born too — from wave holograms to digital pixels and illustrative scenes. Each tells a slightly different story.
petrolPay.brandAppsLede: Shown here is the primary direction of the illustration with windmills and an electric car. Just before launch the partner logo was swapped to Visa, but I'm showing the primary version because it felt aesthetically stronger and best captures the brand's character.
petrolPay.portalTitle: PKPK — three clicks to a card.
petrolPay.portalLede: Responsive web portal for ordering a Petrol Pay card. SVG animations, a short intro video, intuitive flow from welcome to confirmation.

mbills.heroLede: Five years. One system. Three redesigns, four cards and an award that still holds up. This is the portfolio of a brand that never sat still.
mbills.metaRoleValue: Branding · UX/UI · Cards · TV & Print · Photo
mbills.relationshipTitle:
Five years of work with one team,

across many products.
mbills.relationshipBody1: mBills is the Slovenian mobile wallet — one of the few local fintechs that left a mark across Europe. I worked with them from late 2019 to 2024: the first end-to-end UX/UI redesign in 2019 brought home a FinTech Award.
mbills.relationshipBody2: In between: a full brand refresh in 2023, a family of cards (mBills Mastercard, Spaycy ghost card, Wish + Vibe prepaid collection), TV ads, posters, photography, POS design and back-end systems.
mbills.relationshipBody3: The last wallet redesign in 2024 went into development, but never on air — the new owner is shutting it down this year. It's here anyway, because it's the work I'm most proud of.
mbills.metaCellRoleValue:
Brand identity

UX & UI

Card design

TV & print

Photo direction
mbills.timeline1Desc: UX/UI redesign that brought home the FinTech Award.
mbills.timeline2Desc: Campaigns, photography, POS and back-end systems.
mbills.timeline3Desc: Full brand identity — a new visual language.
mbills.timeline4Desc: Mastercard, Spaycy ghost, Wish & Vibe prepaid.
mbills.timeline5Desc: The most beautiful redesign. In the code, not in the wild.
mbills.chapter1Lede: The first end-to-end UX/UI redesign of the mBills wallet. From useful to useful + beautiful. When I joined, the team told me to draw the app I'd want for myself — so my mockups included a piggy-bank, shared account, loyalty profile and a crypto marketplace. Not all of it shipped, but a lot did. Along the way I translated every crypto white paper and coin description into Slovenian. The part that shipped earned industry recognition.
mbills.awardSub: For the leading mobile wallet in the region, 2019.
mbills.awardQuote: Beyond the UX execution, Tina actively contributed content concepts for innovative product ideas — among them the shared account, loyalty program, and other features adding value for users and the business.
mbills.chapter2Lede: Full brand refresh — softer, more modern, no loss of recognition.
mbills.marketingLede: Collaboration on TV ads, plus print campaigns, social visuals and POS materials.
mbills.marketing1Desc: Creative collaboration for the national TV networks.
mbills.marketing2Desc: Jumbo posters, magazine ads, brochures, banner networks.
mbills.marketing3Desc: Photography for campaigns, social, packshots and store windows.
mbills.cardsLede: Mastercard for every day. Spaycy ghost for digital payments. Wish and Vibe — a prepaid collection that glows in the dark.
mbills.crownTitle: Gift cards that glow in the dark.
mbills.crownBody: People feel a bit guilty handing over a gift card — like they ran out of ideas. Wish and Vibe were born as a funny gift — actually first with events in mind: comic-strip packaging instead of a dry sleeve, neon colours and glow-in-the-dark print that turn the card into a hit at any club, festival or company night. No one in the world makes cards like these.
mbills.webLede: The main mBills portal as the sales surface and info hub — plus a separate Wish card portal where the buyer checks balance and meets the brand's comic universe.
mbills.chapter4Lede: More modern, lighter, with 3D animations and soft transitions. The design was done, the code was written — the user never saw it.
mbills.epitaphBody: In 2024 Petrol became the new owner of mBills. This year the brand is being sunset and the product direction has shifted elsewhere. The new wallet stayed forever in the repository and in my portfolio. Sometimes the best work is the work the world never sees.

cookies.text: This website uses cookies for analytics (Google Analytics) to understand how visitors use the site. Data is anonymous.
